description: Capturing self strongly in an escaping closure stored on the object, creating a retain cycle that leaks the object.
body: Failure mode: an object stores an escaping closure (completion handler, Combine sink, timer) that captures self strongly, so self and the closure keep each other alive and deinit never runs. Detection signals: Instruments Leaks/Allocations shows the object's count never dropping; deinit logging never fires; memory grows as screens are revisited. Do not capture self strongly in a closure the object itself retains. Safer fallback: use [weak self] (guard let self = self else { return }) or [unowned self] only when lifetime is guaranteed, break the cycle, and verify deinit runs and Leaks is clean.
